summary: JPEG image colors change (particularly blues become brighter) after decoding with SKBitmap.Decode and re-encoding with SKPixmap.Encode, caused by Skia's CMYK/YCCK JPEG handling and SKBitmap losing colorspace information for backwards compatibility.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Color of jpeg image changed after decode/encode — blue becomes brighter

stepsToReproduce: Open a JPEG file (especially CMYK/YCCK encoded JPEGs from Photoshop) as FileStream
Compare input and output images in Windows Photos, Safari, or Photoshop
Observe blue channel appears brighter/different; only Chrome/Chromium renders the output correctly

attachments.description: Output JPEG after SKBitmap.Decode + SKPixmap.Encode (colors shifted)
Second set of input/output images by reporter (Sept 2020)
Input and output produced by 2.84.0-preview.2 beta
Another JPEG set showing the issue (Mar 2021)

repoLinks.description: Skia source comment explaining inverted CMYK convention for Photoshop JPEGs
How to determine JPEG color space
Skia discuss thread on CMYK JPEG handling

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Issue still active in 2026 with users confirming the problem persists; the root cause is in upstream Skia's CMYK JPEG codec behavior that only Chromium-based viewers understand.

analysis.summary: JPEG decode/re-encode color shift is caused by two interrelated issues: (1) SKBitmap.Decode loses the JPEG's embedded colorspace metadata for backwards compatibility, and (2) Skia treats CMYK/YCCK JPEGs as Photoshop inverted CMYK during decoding, producing a result that only Chromium renders correctly. Most other image viewers (Windows Photos, Safari, Photoshop) display the output with visually different colors.
analysis.rationale: The reporter provides working code, sample images, and clear before/after comparison. Multiple users confirmed the bug across platforms and SkiaSharp versions through 2026. Maintainer (mattleibow) confirmed SKBitmap loses colorspace for backwards compatibility. A contributor (ziriax) identified the root cause as CMYK/YCCK JPEG handling with Photoshop-inverted CMYK — a decision made in upstream Skia to match Chromium behavior but incompatible with most other viewers.

keySignals.interpretation: Classic symptom of CMYK channel inversion — blue channel is inflated during CMYK-to-RGB conversion

keySignals.text: The bitmap loses it's colorspace information for backwards compatibility.
keySignals.interpretation: SKBitmap.Decode intentionally strips colorspace — confirmed root cause in the C# wrapper layer

keySignals.text: only Google Chromium display the Google Skia output correctly
keySignals.interpretation: Output is encoded with Skia's inverted-CMYK JPEG format — only Chromium understands it correctly; this is upstream Skia's deliberate choice

keySignals.text: Color Transform : Unknown (RGB or CMYK) [from ExifTool]
keySignals.interpretation: Input JPEG is CMYK or YCCK — triggers Skia's Photoshop inverted CMYK conversion path

keySignals.text: when this issue can be fixed?
keySignals.interpretation: Still actively affecting users as of 2026; issue has not been resolved

codeInvestigation.finding: SKBitmap.Decode(SKCodec) reads codec.Info which includes the JPEG's embedded colorspace, then passes it to Decode(codec, info). In theory colorspace is preserved here, but maintainer confirmed it is stripped for backwards compatibility — this likely occurs at the C API or Skia native layer when targeting legacy color types.

All SKImageInfo constructors except the 5-argument form set ColorSpace = null. When a caller passes a bitmapInfo without an explicit colorspace, the decoded bitmap has no associated colorspace, causing color management to be skipped during re-encoding.

SKImage.FromEncodedData wraps sk_image_new_from_encoded which defers decoding, preserving the encoded data and its color profile. Maintainer suggested this as a potential workaround because it avoids the SKBitmap colorspace loss.

workarounds: Use SKImage.FromEncodedData instead of SKBitmap.Decode — preserves encoded color profile and defers decode, which may avoid the CMYK→RGB conversion loss

Use SKCodec directly and request decode into an explicit SKImageInfo with ColorSpace set to SKColorSpace.CreateSrgb() — forces sRGB conversion at decode time rather than relying on null colorspace passthrough
Inspect the input JPEG's EXIF color transform before processing; if CMYK/YCCK, use a dedicated CMYK library (e.g., ImageSharp) for that specific format
nextQuestions: Does using SKImage.FromEncodedData + SKImage.Encode produce the correct output across all viewers?
Does explicitly passing WithColorSpace(SKColorSpace.CreateSrgb()) to SKBitmap.Decode(codec, info) fix the issue for sRGB JPEGs while failing for CMYK?
Is this reproducible only with CMYK/YCCK JPEGs, or does it also affect standard sRGB JPEGs?
Has Skia upstream changed CMYK JPEG handling in milestones after m87 that SkiaSharp has since adopted?

resolution.hypothesis: The color shift affects CMYK/YCCK JPEGs because Skia uses a Photoshop-specific inverted CMYK convention understood only by Chromium. Additionally, SKBitmap strips the original colorspace for backwards compatibility. Combined, re-encoding produces a JPEG that only Chrome displays correctly.

proposals.title: Use SKImage.FromEncodedData instead of SKBitmap.Decode
proposals.description: SKImage.FromEncodedData defers decoding and preserves the encoded color profile, avoiding the SKBitmap colorspace loss. This is the maintainer-recommended approach for preserving color fidelity.

proposals.title: Explicitly set sRGB colorspace when decoding
proposals.description: Pass codec.Info.WithColorSpace(SKColorSpace.CreateSrgb()) to SKBitmap.Decode so the decoder performs an explicit sRGB conversion, avoiding null colorspace passthrough. This works for standard sRGB JPEGs but may not fix CMYK/YCCK inputs.

proposals.title: Fix SKBitmap.Decode to preserve colorspace by default
proposals.description: Remove or make opt-out the backwards-compat colorspace stripping in SKBitmap.Decode. This is a breaking change for users relying on legacy null-colorspace behavior.

resolution.recommendedReason: Non-breaking, immediately available, and aligns with maintainer's own suggestion.

actionability.reason: Confirmed wrong-output bug affecting multiple users through 2026. Root cause is understood (CMYK JPEG + colorspace loss) but the exact fix path requires deeper investigation of SKBitmap colorspace handling and whether SKImage.FromEncodedData provides a complete workaround.

actions.description: Apply bug, SkiaSharp core, platform, and backend labels
Explain root cause and provide workaround using SKImage.FromEncodedData
